text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon in sleepy Willowtree Village, Farmer James tended to his chickens late into dusk. Birds chimed their evening tunes while he checked roofs for rooks. His favourite birdie was a wild goose he named Grace—who seemed forever aloft.

One evening, James noticed something unusual. Grace wouldn’t glide down. Instead, she’d circle high overhead before suddenly darting south faster than any goose couldASFOWL!

Concern turned curiosity.

Jim traced her path south. Along the branches of trees whispered secrets.

Past Willow Creek.

Across ancient oak roots.

Towards distant mountains.

They flew over peaks where butterflies danced.

Farther still.

Along rivers darker than midnight.

At last Grace swooped down into Farmer Martha’s plum garden.

“There you are!” Martha cried.

She’d lost chickens among plum trees!

James sighed.

“We’ll just have to lay bait,” said Martha.

That night James hung hooks through bait. Grace plucked treasures beneath moonlit oceans.

Days flew.

Grace taught James
